steps.title: Isolate the $b$-term
steps.content: Start from $a = \frac{5}{13}b + 10$. Subtract 10 from both sides: $$a - 10 = \frac{5}{13}b$$

steps.title: Solve for $b$
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $\frac{13}{5}$ (the reciprocal of $\frac{5}{13}$): $$b = \frac{13}{5}(a - 10)$$ Distribute: $$b = \frac{13}{5}a - \frac{13}{5} \cdot 10 = \frac{13}{5}a - 26$$

steps.title: Compare to each choice
steps.content:
• A) $b = \frac{5}{13}a + 10$ — just swaps variables, wrong coefficient and sign; eliminate.
  • B) $b = \frac{5}{13}a - 26$ — correct constant but wrong slope ($\frac{5}{13}$ vs $\frac{13}{5}$); eliminate.
  • C) $b = \frac{13}{5}a - 10$ — correct slope but wrong constant ($-10$ vs $-26$); eliminate.
  • D) $b = \frac{13}{5}a - 26$ — matches the derived expression exactly.
That confirms D is correct.
